title: Tesla earnings recap: Stock drops 4% after EV maker misses big on profit
byline: Steven Tweedie, Lloyd Lee, Ben Shimkus, Rya Jetha, Tom Carter, Samuel O'Brient, Max Adams
excerpt: Tesla's record Q2 vehicle deliveries helped drive $28.24 billion in revenue, but EPS came in far lower than expected. The stock slide 4% after hours.
content_html:
Updated 2026-07-22T22:52:22.593Z

Read in app

Tesla stock slipped more than 4% after hours as the EV maker delivered revenue ahead of estimates but profit that missed Wall Street's expectations.

Tesla's record Q2 vehicle deliveries helped drive $28.24 billion in revenue, topping analysts' estimates of $26.31 billion. Quarterly profit was far below expectations, with adjusted EPS of 33 cents versus estimates of 50 cents.

"Q2 was a strong quarter for our core vehicle, energy and services businesses as well as our manufacturing, infrastructure and AI initiatives," the company said in its earnings release.

The second-quarter report is Tesla's first since Elon Musk's other big company went public last month.

On an analyst call following the results, Musk stayed mum on SpaceX-Tesla merger chatter, but highlighted synergies between the two companies. Bulls say that a tie-up of the two companies is a natural fit and would streamline some of Musk's goals.

Tesla expects to spend more than ever in the coming quarter. The company said that it is in its "largest and most exciting period of investment." Musk said that Tesla should be spending on capex "as fast as we can without being too wasteful."

Musk also sought to temper expectations for the production of Tesla's humanoid robot, Optimus, saying it will have a flat, long start to its production S-curve. He stressed manufacturing the robot is extremely challenging.

On the robotaxi front, Tesla's executives said a careful rollout was key to avoiding any incidents that could damage the technology's reputation. They said Tesla has completed 380,000 miles of unsupervised robotaxi rides with zero "notable incidents."

Musk and other Tesla executives also talked about supply chain constraints (Micron got a Musk shout-out for allocating memory chips during the shortage), Cybercab, and Semi production on the analyst call — follow along for the play-by-play:

But that's changed since the Trump administration has canceled that regulatory device. This quarter, Tesla earned $146 million in credits, down from $380 million last quarter and $439 million a year earlier.

Cantor's bullish target rooted in Tesla's robotaxi business

Cantor Fitzgerald has a $510 price target for Tesla stock, 35% higher than Wednesday's price. Analyst Andres Sheppard highlighted the latest vehicle deliveries beat and successful robotaxi rollout, noting that his team expects to see progress continue.

"Overall, we continue to see Tesla's Robotaxi segment and Cybercab as a software-as-a-service, high-margin model," he noted. "We continue to expect TSLA will have the ability to scale rapidly following commercialization (despite the delayed expansion) and capture meaningful market share."

Auto revenues are increasingly irrelevant for the stock, Barclays says

Barclays is less bullish on Tesla stock than some Wall Street peers, maintaining a $370 price target and equal weight rating. While its analysts noted that the company's automotive business is improving, they raised the question of how much that actually matters going forward.

"We believe the key message from the market with respect to Tesla's frothy multiple has been that Tesla's current revenue stream (which is predominantly automotive) is increasingly irrelevant, with the key focus for the stock on future mega-growth initiatives in AI - Robotaxi /FSD / autonomous driving + bots / Optimus humanoids," Barclays wrote.

"We think focus for earnings will remain on TSLA's robotaxi deployments," BofA wrote. "TSLA added an additional market & is scaling TX fleet. We think core auto business remains healthy with reported 2Q deliveries above Street as TSLA gains global BEV market share."

blocks.html_en: Lars Moravy, Tesla's VP of vehicle engineering, says New Jersey's recent legislative proposal on autonomous cars was a "little disheartening."

sentences.en: Lars Moravy, Tesla's VP of vehicle engineering, says New Jersey's recent legislative proposal on autonomous cars was a "little disheartening."
sentences.zh: 特斯拉汽车工程副总裁拉尔斯·莫拉维 (Lars Moravy) 表示，新泽西州最近关于自动驾驶汽车的立法提案“有点令人沮丧”。

blocks.html_en: The state law, which has yet to pass, would require autonomous cars to have two additional sensor modalities, such as lidar and radar, on top of cameras. That would essentially ban Tesla from operating self-driving cars in the state unless they made significant changes to their vehicles. (Musk has been vocally against lidar.)

sentences.en: The state law, which has yet to pass, would require autonomous cars to have two additional sensor modalities, such as lidar and radar, on top of cameras.
sentences.zh: 尚未通过的州法律将要求自动驾驶汽车在摄像头顶部配备两种额外的传感器模式，例如激光雷达和雷达。

sentences.en: That would essentially ban Tesla from operating self-driving cars in the state unless they made significant changes to their vehicles.
sentences.zh: 这实际上将禁止特斯拉在该州运营自动驾驶汽车，除非他们对车辆进行重大改变。

sentences.en: (Musk has been vocally against lidar.)
sentences.zh: （马斯克一直公开反对激光雷达。）

特斯拉股价盘后下跌逾 4%，因为这家电动汽车制造商的营收超出预期，但利润低于华尔街预期。

特斯拉第二季度创纪录的汽车交付量推动收入达到 282.4 亿美元，超过分析师预期的 263.1 亿美元。 季度利润远低于预期，调整后每股收益为 33 美分，而预期为 50 美分。

该公司在财报中表示：“第二季度是我们核心汽车、能源和服务业务以及制造、基础设施和人工智能计划的强劲季度。”

这是自埃隆·马斯克的另一家大公司上个月上市以来特斯拉发布的第一份第二季度报告。

在财报公布后的分析师电话会议上，马斯克对 SpaceX 与特斯拉合并的传闻保持沉默，但强调了两家公司之间的协同效应。 看多者表示，两家公司的合作是天作之合，将简化马斯克的一些目标。
